author: PC Gamer
text:
Roblox wants AI to make its games photorealistic, but the devs making those games aren't sold on the idea: 'I don't think that your average player right now wants to do that'
Games like 99 Nights in the Forest appeal to kids because of the way they look, not in spite of it.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Steam week in review: Forza Horizon is the last truly mainstream multiplatform racing series
All the interesting Steam facts for the week ending May 10.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Friendslop approaches total cultural victory as co-op hit Gamble With Your Friends sells 1 million copies in one week
Let's go gambling!

author: PC Gamer
text:
This Team Fortress 2 zombie mod looks so good, we almost thought it was official
Left 4 Dead 3 or Mann vs. Machine 2?

author: PC Gamer
text:
I went back to Grand Theft Auto 4 for a Call of Duty zombie mod mash up and came away wondering how the heck I got this old
New York state of (losing my) mind.

author: PC Gamer
text:
What are the best and worst lyrics you've ever heard in a videogame song?
Bars or barf?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Darkest Dungeon studio refuses to AI generate deceased narrator's voice despite his permission: 'I would never, ever erode his incredible and timeless performances'
Many fall in the face of chaos, but not this one. Not today.

author: PC Gamer
text:
An unofficial PC port of The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess just released
Twilight Princess has escaped its containment on Nintendo consoles.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Bennett Foddy, designer of QWOP and Baby Steps, is obsessed with friendslop games and won't uninstall Baldur's Gate 3, even though he'll probably never finish it: 'It was too big and so I stopped'
The master of bizarre control schemes talks us through the games on his PC.

author: PC Gamer
text:
The Sims 3 review (2009)
Love, work and kleptomania return in The Sims 3, Electronic Arts' enormous life-controlling sequel to the bestselling PC game of all time.

author: PC Gamer
text:
This artist 'brings hair to life' by painting video game scenes on the back of wigs, and yes, it really is as impressive as it sounds
And to think I found haircare to be tedious.

author: PC Gamer
text:
No, Richard Dawkins, AI is bloody well not conscious
And if you think otherwise, you'd better at least attempt to don your philosophy cap.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Two Point Museum's latest DLC features a buildable studio where artists can create 'millions' of unique artworks, and there's 'no generative AI involved' whatsoever
Two Point Museum: Arty Facts is available now.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Rust's latest update adds mortars but 'no visual guide to help you land shells,' so get ready for some chaos
"Firing a mortar requires mastery through trial & error."

author: PC Gamer
text:
The next World of Darkness game to be revealed is a Werewolf metroidvania, and I respect the hustle
They've led by showing gameplay, let's see how that plays out for them.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Riven co-creator defends his use of AI art: 'Any artist can take a brush and, without thinking, an artist can create slop'
The vinyl soundtrack's release should have been a moment to celebrate, instead it's been marked by controversy.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Stellaris is 10 years old and still getting DLC, which is impressive
We talked to some devs about how the space GSG has maintained such a long orbit.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Stellaris is getting space nomads and scenarios for its 10th birthday
Get ready to live out your Battlestar dreams.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Pearl Abyss marketing director says Crimson Desert's rapid 'live service' patch cadence is business as usual for an MMO studio: 'That is not normal in the industry. That is normal here'
"The DNA of the company is inherent in listening to players."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Fallout co-creator Tim Cain once proposed a first-person time-travel RPG where you could assassinate historical figures and create paradoxes
"One, visit 15 different time periods. Two, meet interesting historical figures. Three, kill them."

author: PC Gamer
text:
Final Fantasy 14 player harnesses the power of furniture slots to make their in-game house look like Pragmata
"Increased placement limit is the best!"

author: PC Gamer
text:
If you've ever had your shoes stolen in Kingdom Come Deliverance 2, that was apparently PC Gamer's fault, sorry
Chris Livingston's fault specifically.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Roblox wants AI to make its games photorealistic, but the devs making those games aren't sold on the idea: 'I don't think that your average player right now wants to do that'
Games like 99 Nights in the Forest appeal to kids because of the way they look, not in spite of it.

text:
Steam week in review: Forza Horizon is the last truly mainstream multiplatform racing series
All the interesting Steam facts for the week ending May 10.

author: PC Gamer
text:
If you've ever had your shoes stolen in Kingdom Come Deliverance 2, that was apparently PC Gamer's fault, sorry
Chris Livingston's fault specifically.

author: PC Gamer
text:
'Apple does this and y’all love it': Microsoft defends new Low Latency feature in Windows 11
The new mode is reportedly snappier.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Turns out you yearn for the return of PC Gamer's two favourite genres just as much as we do
PC gamers just want to stack boxes and send little guys to battle.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Battlefield 6's first truly big map feels like playing a different, much better Battlefield
I was burnt out on Battlefield 6, but Golmud is reeling me back in.

author: PC Gamer
text:
You're not paranoid—KCD2's devs gave Henry photo mode voicelines just to stop you abusing it for stealth runs
The hungry lines are just hungry lines, though. And god bless them.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Mixtape review
Perfect playlist, but just a decent videogame.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Directive 8020 review: One giant leap for sci-fi body horror
An unmissable adventure for any fan of sci-fi horror.

author: PC Gamer
text:
After fining Google more money than exists on Earth, Russia demands so little from EA it feels like a weird joke
Well one of these strategies has gotta work.

author: PC Gamer
text:
New Steam update hints at four separate Steam Machine models and a reservation system
Presumably that's for different specced models.

author: PC Gamer
text:
How does co-op work in Outbound?
You'll have to unlock multiplayer before a friend can join the ride.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Mortal Kombat 2 redeems itself by fixing the worst thing about the first movie
As a Mortal Kombat 1 hater, I was pleasantly surprised by its sequel.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Elder Scrolls 6 is a ways off, but who needs it? The world's most specific game jam is back with 40 playable entries based off Oblivion's wonderful/awful persuade-o-wheel
The wheel has come full circle.

author: PC Gamer
text:
Keychron calls this 'the next gen mouse switch' and I believe it: optical and magnetic all-in-one
Rapid trigger or traditional optical clicks: Keychron says why not both?

author: PC Gamer
text:
Drawquarium is one of the most relaxing sandbox games I've played in a hot minute, even if it did remind me I have zero artistic skill
Stop by Any Fin Goes, I hear it's a great aquarium with very well-drawn fish.
